name: vol_floor_wins_no_squeeze
description: safe_vol * dynamic_multiplier (0.1 * 1.5 = 0.15) is BELOW the dynamic_min_stop floor (0.30), so the max() clamp returns dynamic_min_stop. With para_armed=False and breakeven_locked=False, no squeeze multiplier is applied. Pins the 'floor wins' branch of the max() AND the no-squeeze branch of the conditional.
derivation: symphony_vol > 0, so safe_vol = symphony_vol = 0.1. raw = safe_vol * dynamic_multiplier = 0.1 * 1.5 = 0.15. active = max(raw, dynamic_min_stop) = max(0.15, 0.30) = 0.30. Neither flag is armed -> no squeeze -> output = 0.30. Note: parabolic_squeeze_multiplier=0.5 is supplied but MUST be ignored because both flags are False; if a wrong impl multiplied unconditionally, the output would be 0.15 (catches that bug).
